name: Mega Evolution — Ascended Heroes Elite Trainer Box
description: The flagship ETB from 2026's first and largest set ever (295 cards). 9 booster packs, the full-art N's Zekrom promo, 65 sleeves, 40 energy, dice and coin. Case-fresh.

name: Ascended Heroes Pokémon Center Elite Trainer Box
description: Pokémon Center exclusive ETB with 11 packs (2 extra) and two N's Zekrom promos, one bearing the PC logo variant. Limited Special Set distribution makes this a strong hold.

name: Ascended Heroes Booster Bundle (6 Packs)
description: Six sealed Ascended Heroes booster packs. Rip for the new Mega Attack Rares, the TCG-debut Mega Dragonite ex, or Team Rocket's Mewtwo ex.

name: Ascended Heroes Premium Poster Collection — Mega Lucario
description: 10 booster packs, a full-art foil promo, and a large Mega Lucario poster. One of the scarcer Special-Set products.

name: Prismatic Evolutions Elite Trainer Box
description: The Eevee-evolution set that broke the internet — home of the four-figure Umbreon ex chase. Notoriously hard to find sealed. Never searched or weighed.

name: Prismatic Evolutions Booster Bundle
description: Sealed booster bundle from the Stellar Tera Eeveelution set. A collector favorite with sky-high demand.

name: Surging Sparks Booster Box
description: 36-pack sealed box from the final S&V main expansion — 250+ cards, ACE SPEC returns, and the coveted Pikachu ex Special Illustration Rare. Case-fresh.

name: Journey Together Elite Trainer Box
description: Trainer's Pokémon return — N, Lillie, Iono and Hop headline. Sealed ETB with 9 packs and the exclusive promo.

name: Destined Rivals Booster Box
description: Team Rocket returns in one of the most stunning S&V sets. Sealed 36-pack box — never searched. A strong sealed hold.

name: Temporal Forces Booster Box
description: Sealed 36-pack box featuring the debut of Ace Spec trainers and the striking Iron/Ancient Paradox lineup. Case-fresh, never weighed.

name: Twilight Masquerade Elite Trainer Box
description: Home of Ogerpon ex and the Bloodmoon Ursaluna chase. Sealed ETB with 9 packs, sleeves and the exclusive promo.

name: Shrouded Fable Elite Trainer Box
description: A hot special set built around Pecharunt and shiny chases. Smaller print run than main sets — a strong sealed pickup.

name: Stellar Crown Booster Box
description: Sealed 36-pack box headlined by Terapagos ex and gorgeous Illustration Rares. Case-fresh.

name: Paldean Fates Elite Trainer Box
description: The shiny-Pokémon special set — Shiny Charizard ex and Mew ex chases. High demand, limited allocation. Sealed.

name: Paradox Rift Booster Box
description: Sealed 36-pack box with Roaring Moon ex, Iron Valiant ex and standout Special Illustration Rares. Never searched.

name: Obsidian Flames Booster Box
description: Charizard ex headlines this sealed 36-pack box. A perennial favorite thanks to the Charizard chase. Case-fresh.

name: Crown Zenith Elite Trainer Box
description: Sealed Crown Zenith ETB with 10 packs, sleeves, dice, and the Galarian Gallery insert. Smoke-free, climate-controlled storage.

name: Scarlet & Violet 151 Booster Box
description: 36-pack sealed booster box of the wildly popular 151 set. Case-fresh, never searched or weighed.

name: Hidden Fates Elite Trainer Box
description: Highly sought-after Hidden Fates ETB. A modern grail for sealed collectors. Sealed and authenticated.

name: Evolving Skies Booster Box
description: The Alt-Art king. Sealed Evolving Skies box — home of the chase Umbreon VMAX. Investment-grade sealed product.

name: 1st Edition Base Set Booster Box — Sealed
description: The ultimate sealed grail — a 1999 1st Edition Base Set booster box, factory sealed with authentication. Museum-grade investment piece. Serious inquiries only.

name: Mega Dragonite ex (Mega Attack Rare) — PSA 10
description: The historic TCG debut of Mega Dragonite, in the brand-new pop-art Mega Attack Rare rarity. Graded PSA 10 Gem Mint. One of the defining chase cards of 2026.

name: Team Rocket's Mewtwo ex (SIR) — PSA 10
description: The ultimate villain combo — Team Rocket's Mewtwo ex Special Illustration Rare, PSA 10. Massive crossover appeal from Ascended Heroes.

name: Umbreon ex (Special Illustration Rare) — PSA 10
description: The most hyped modern chase card — Prismatic Evolutions Umbreon ex SIR, graded PSA 10. The card that defined the Prismatic craze.

name: Iono (Special Illustration Rare) — PSA 10
description: One of the most beloved trainer SIRs ever printed — Iono from Paldea Evolved, graded PSA 10. Perennial chase-card demand.

name: Mew ex (Special Illustration Rare) — PSA 10
description: The adorable Mew ex SIR from 151 in a PSA 10 slab — a fan-favorite modern chase with strong Kanto nostalgia.

name: Pikachu ex (Special Illustration Rare) — PSA 10
description: The stunning Pikachu ex SIR — the flagship chase of Surging Sparks — graded PSA 10 Gem Mint.

name: Terapagos ex (Special Illustration Rare) — PSA 10
description: The Stellar Crown mascot Terapagos ex SIR, PSA 10 — crisp centering and a beautiful crystalline illustration.

name: Umbreon VMAX Alt Art ("Moonbreon") — PSA 10
description: The legendary "Moonbreon" — Umbreon VMAX Alternate Art Secret from Evolving Skies, PSA 10. Arguably the most iconic modern alt-art ever printed.

name: Charizard ex (Special Illustration Rare) — PSA 10
description: The gorgeous 151 Charizard ex SIR in a PSA 10 slab — a modern grail with timeless Kanto appeal.

name: Giratina V Alt Art — PSA 10
description: The stunning Giratina V Alternate Art from Lost Origin, graded PSA 10. A community-favorite alt-art and reliable blue-chip modern slab.

name: Charizard — Base Set — PSA 9
description: The holy grail. 1999 Base Set Holo Charizard graded PSA 9 Mint. Sharp corners, clean holo, dead-centered. Fresh from a trusted PSA submission.

name: Charizard — 1st Edition Base Set — PSA 8
description: The card that started it all — 1st Edition (shadowless) Base Set Charizard, PSA 8. The most iconic trading card in the hobby. Full authentication provided.

name: Charizard — Base Set Shadowless — PSA 8
description: Unlimited-run Shadowless Charizard in PSA 8 — the sought-after early print with no drop shadow. A vintage cornerstone.

name: Dark Charizard — Team Rocket — PSA 9
description: Dark Charizard Holo from the 2000 Team Rocket set, PSA 9. A fan-favorite villain-era Charizard variant.

name: Blastoise — 1st Edition Base Set — PSA 8
description: 1st Edition Base Set Holo Blastoise, PSA 8. One of the toughest and most desirable vintage 1st Ed holos to find in high grade.

name: Rayquaza Gold Star — EX Deoxys — PSA 8
description: The legendary Rayquaza Gold Star from EX Deoxys, PSA 8 — one of the most coveted Gold Star cards ever printed.

name: Blaine's Charizard — Gym Challenge — PSA 8
description: Blaine's Charizard Holo from Gym Challenge, PSA 8 — a scarce and beloved Gym-era Charizard for the vintage collector.

name: Espeon — Neo Discovery 1st Edition — PSA 9
description: 1st Edition Espeon Holo from Neo Discovery, PSA 9 — a gorgeous and hard-to-find vintage Eeveelution holo.

name: Lugia — Neo Genesis — PSA 8
description: First-edition Neo Genesis Holo Lugia in a PSA 8 slab. A cornerstone vintage holo for any serious collection.

name: Blastoise — Base Set — PSA 9
description: 1999 Base Set Holo Blastoise, PSA 9 Mint. Beautiful holo bleed and razor-sharp centering.

name: Venusaur — Base Set — PSA 9
description: 1999 Base Set Holo Venusaur, PSA 9 Mint. Completes the Kanto starter trio alongside our Charizard and Blastoise slabs.

name: Umbreon Gold Star — CGC 8.5
description: Ultra-rare POP Series 5 Umbreon Gold Star, graded CGC 8.5. One of the most iconic Eeveelution chase cards ever printed.

name: Charizard — Base Set — PSA 10
description: A true trophy. Unlimited Base Set Charizard in the near-unobtainable PSA 10 Gem Mint grade. One of the crown jewels of the hobby. Full provenance provided.

name: Crystal Charizard — Skyridge — PSA 10
description: The famed Crystal Charizard from Skyridge — the final WOTC-era set — in PSA 10. Exceptionally rare in Gem Mint. A vintage grail.

name: Trophy Pikachu (No. 3 Trainer) — PSA 9
description: A genuine Trophy Pikachu (No. 3 Trainer) awarded at a 1990s Japanese tournament, PSA 9. Extraordinarily rare tournament-prize card with full authentication.

name: Tropical Wind (Tropical Mega Battle) — PSA 8
description: The Tropical Wind prize card from the 1999 Tropical Mega Battle — one of the rarest and most storied promos in the hobby. PSA 8 with authentication.

name: Pikachu Illustrator — PSA 7
description: The most famous Pokémon card in existence. 1998 Pikachu Illustrator promo, PSA 7. Full authentication paperwork and provenance provided. Serious inquiries only.
